sections.paragraph:
& מַעֲכָת Jos 13:13 (“oppression”), [Maachah], pr.n.-

(1) of a town and region at the foot of Hermon, near Geshur, a district of Syria (see גְּשׁוּר and גְּשׁוּרִי No. 1 ), 2Sa 10:6, 2Sa 10:8 Deu 3:13. Hence the neighbouring tract of Syria was called אֲרַם מַעֲכָה 1Ch 19:6. Gent. noun מַעֲכָתִי Deu 3:14 Jos 12:5 13:11 2Ki 25:23. Comp. בֵּית מַעֲכָה and אָבֵל בֵּית מַעֲכָה.

(2) of several men-(a) 1Ki 2:39 1Ch 11:43 27:16 called also מָעֹוךְ, which see.-(b) Gen 22:24 where however the sex is doubtful;-and of women, as-(c) the wife of Rehoboam, 1Ki 15:2 10:13 2Ch 11:20 called also, 13:2 , מִיכָיָהוּ.-(d) 2Sa 3:3.-(e) 1Ch 2:48.-(f) 1Ch 7:15, 16 1Ch 7:16.
